name: Fault tree analysis

problemSignals.description: A specific undesirable system event requires deductive decomposition.
Failure may require combinations of lower-level events.

requiredEvidence.description: A precise observable top event with system boundary and operating condition.
Component functions, interfaces, failure evidence, and independence assumptions.

assumptions: Boolean gate semantics are adequate for the modeled event

deterministicSteps.action: Fix the top event, system boundary, operating state, and success/failure semantics.
Deductively expand causes with explicit AND/OR gates until justified basic events or stopping rules.
Derive minimal cut sets and identify unsupported independence or probability assumptions.

outputs.description: Unambiguous failure event and analysis boundary.
Traceable Boolean causal tree with stopping rules.
Minimal cut sets and assumption gaps.

whenAll: Top event and boundaries are observable
Basic events and dependencies are evidenced
Some basic-event rates or independence assumptions are unverified
Causal structure cannot support gate selection

contraindications: The analysis question is exploratory rather than a defined top event
Temporal or continuous dynamics invalidate Boolean-event modeling

contract: Preserve gate semantics and evidence links

inScope: Deductive analysis of a defined undesirable event
